attrs.alt: Entra ID Provisioning Log flow and export destinations

content.text: Microsoft Entra ID Provisioning Logs record every user lifecycle event that flows through the Entra provisioning engine: inbound HR-driven provisioning (Workday, SAP SuccessFactors → Entra ID), outbound app provisioning (Entra ID → Salesforce, GitHub, ServiceNow), and on-premises write-back via Entra Cloud Sync. Each log entry records the action taken (Create, Update, Delete, Disable), the result (Success, Failure, Skipped, Quarantine), the source and target identity, and every attribute that was read or written.
Provisioning Logs are separate from Audit Logs. Audit Logs record admin changes in the Entra portal; Provisioning Logs record automated identity sync operations driven by the provisioning agent.
The AADProvisioningLogs table in Log Analytics exposes the following key fields.
UTC time of the provisioning cycle step
UPN, objectId, type in the source system
UPN/ID, type in the target system
App registration (service principal) that owns the provisioning job
Provisioning job ID — links entries across a sync cycle
Unique identifier for each incremental sync cycle
Tenant — useful for multi-tenant Sentinel workspaces
Object was created, updated, or deleted in the target
Attribute conflict, API throttle, permission denied
Object deliberately excluded from this cycle
Scoping filter, already in-scope but no delta, soft-match found
Job paused for this object after repeated failures
Persistent error requiring manual admin intervention
Object is in-flight; result not yet determined
Transient — check again in next cycle
Reading Provisioning Logs via Graph API
The /auditLogs/provisioning endpoint supports $filter on status, action, and activityDateTime. Use it for programmatic investigation and export.

# List provisioning log events (last 7 days)
GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/auditLogs/provisioning
  ?$filter=activityDateTime ge 2024-06-01T00:00:00Z
  &$orderby=activityDateTime desc
  &$top=50

# Filter to failures only
GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/auditLogs/provisioning
  ?$filter=provisioningStatusInfo/status eq 'failure'
  &$select=activityDateTime,action,sourceIdentity,targetIdentity,provisioningStatusInfo

# PowerShell
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All'
Get-MgAuditLogProvisioning -Filter "provisioningStatusInfo/status eq 'failure'" -Top 100 |
  Select-Object ActivityDateTime, Action,
    @{N='Source';E={$_.SourceIdentity.DisplayName}},
    @{N='Status';E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.Status}},
    @{N='ErrorCode';E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.ErrorInformation.ErrorCode}}

Provisioning Logs are a separate diagnostic category from AuditLogs and SignInLogs. Enable it explicitly in the diagnostic setting alongside the other two categories to ensure full identity observability.
Once provisioning logs reach Log Analytics (table name AADProvisioningLogs), use KQL to monitor sync health and investigate individual object failures.

// Provisioning event summary for last 7 days
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize Count = count() by Result, Action
| order by Count desc

// All failures with error codes
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Result == 'Failure'
| extend ErrorCode     = tostring(StatusInfo.errorCode)
| extend ErrorReason   = tostring(StatusInfo.additionalDetails)
| extend SourceUPN     = tostring(SourceIdentity.UserPrincipalName)
| extend TargetApp     = tostring(ProvisioningServicePrincipal.DisplayName)
| project TimeGenerated, Action, SourceUPN, TargetApp, ErrorCode, ErrorReason
| order by TimeGenerated desc
Objects in Quarantine have had their provisioning job paused after repeated failures. They need manual resolution before sync resumes.

// Objects in Quarantine state — provisioning paused due to repeated failures
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where Result == 'Quarantine'
| extend ErrorCode = tostring(StatusInfo.errorCode)
| extend SourceUPN = tostring(SourceIdentity.UserPrincipalName)
| extend App       = tostring(ProvisioningServicePrincipal.DisplayName)
| summarize LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated), Count = count() by SourceUPN, App, ErrorCode
| order by Count desc
Skipped entries are often benign (scoping filter), but unexplained skip reasons can indicate misconfigured attribute mappings or stale filter conditions.

// Skipped objects — understand why accounts are excluded from provisioning
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Result == 'Skipped'
| extend Reason    = tostring(StatusInfo.skipReason)
| extend SourceUPN = tostring(SourceIdentity.UserPrincipalName)
| extend App       = tostring(ProvisioningServicePrincipal.DisplayName)
| summarize Count = count() by Reason, App
| order by Count desc
Track what values the provisioning engine actually wrote to the target system. Useful for verifying HR data quality and debugging attribute mapping rules.

// Attribute changes made during provisioning — audit what was written to target
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Result == 'Success'
| mv-expand ModifiedProperties
| extend AttrName  = tostring(ModifiedProperties.Name)
| extend NewValue  = tostring(ModifiedProperties.NewValue)
| extend OldValue  = tostring(ModifiedProperties.OldValue)
| extend SourceUPN = tostring(SourceIdentity.UserPrincipalName)
| where AttrName in ('UserPrincipalName','mail','department','jobTitle','manager')
| project TimeGenerated, SourceUPN, AttrName, OldValue, NewValue
| order by TimeGenerated desc
Plot success, failure, and skipped counts in hourly buckets to detect degradation over time — a drop in success rate often precedes a full quarantine event.

// Provisioning success rate trend — hourly buckets over last 72h
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(72h)
| summarize
    Success  = countif(Result == 'Success'),
    Failure  = countif(Result == 'Failure'),
    Skipped  = countif(Result == 'Skipped')
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| extend SuccessRate = round(100.0 * Success / (Success + Failure + Skipped), 1)
| order by TimeGenerated asc
Sentinel Analytics Rule: Elevated Error Rate
Alert when any provisioning job's failure rate exceeds 10% in a one-hour window. Low-volume jobs (fewer than 5 events) are excluded to avoid noise from initial setup cycles.

// Sentinel: Alert when provisioning error rate exceeds 10% in any 1-hour window
let window = 1h;
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(window)
| summarize
    Total   = count(),
    Failed  = countif(Result == 'Failure')
    by ProvisioningServicePrincipalId, ProvisioningServicePrincipalDisplayName
| where Total > 5  // ignore low-volume apps
| extend ErrorRate = round(100.0 * Failed / Total, 1)
| where ErrorRate > 10
| project
    App = ProvisioningServicePrincipalDisplayName,
    Total, Failed, ErrorRate
| order by ErrorRate desc
Use the Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK to export all provisioning failures from the last 30 days for offline analysis or compliance reporting.

# Export all provisioning failures from last 30 days to CSV
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All'

$filter = "provisioningStatusInfo/status eq 'failure'" +
          " and activityDateTime ge $(((Get-Date).AddDays(-30)).ToString('o'))"

$events = Get-MgAuditLogProvisioning -Filter $filter -All

$events | Select-Object ActivityDateTime, Action,
    @{N='SourceUPN';    E={$_.SourceIdentity.UserPrincipalName}},
    @{N='TargetApp';    E={$_.ProvisioningServicePrincipal.DisplayName}},
    @{N='ErrorCode';    E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.ErrorInformation.ErrorCode}},
    @{N='ErrorReason';  E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.ErrorInformation.AdditionalDetails}} |
    Export-Csv provisioning-failures.csv -NoTypeInformation

Write-Host "Exported $($events.Count) failure events"
SP lacks permission to write to the target
Check Enterprise App → Provisioning → Credentials; re-grant admin consent
Two target objects match the same source anchor
De-duplicate in target; update matching attribute
Mapped attribute does not exist in target app schema
Refresh app schema; remove or remap the missing attribute
Value violates target app format constraint (e.g. max length)
Add a transformation expression to truncate or reformat the value
Extend provisioning interval; contact app vendor for higher rate limit
Required attribute is null for the user
Fix the source attribute mapping; ensure HR source always exports the field
Provisioning Logs vs Audit Logs vs Sign-In Logs

Provisioning latency is the delay between a change in the source system (e.g. HR database) and the corresponding write to the target (e.g. Salesforce). High P95 latency indicates throttling, large incremental batches, or provisioning agent connectivity issues.

// Measure average provisioning latency per job (time from source change to target write)
// Requires correlating sign-in logs with provisioning events via jobId
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Result == 'Success'
| extend App = tostring(ProvisioningServicePrincipalDisplayName)
| summarize
    AvgLatencyMin = avg(datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, ActivityDateTime)),
    P95LatencyMin = percentile(datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, ActivityDateTime), 95),
    Events = count()
    by App
| order by P95LatencyMin desc
Tracking every new account created via provisioning lets you verify HR-driven onboarding is completing end-to-end and surfaces unauthorized account creation through unexpected provisioning paths.

// Track all new accounts created via provisioning in the last 24 hours
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where Action == 'Create' and Result == 'Success'
| extend SourceUPN  = tostring(SourceIdentity.UserPrincipalName)
| extend TargetUPN  = tostring(TargetIdentity.UserPrincipalName)
| extend TargetApp  = tostring(ProvisioningServicePrincipalDisplayName)
| extend Department = tostring(parse_json(tostring(ModifiedProperties))
    | where Name == 'department' | project NewValue | limit 1)
| project TimeGenerated, SourceUPN, TargetUPN, TargetApp
| order by TimeGenerated desc
On-Demand Provisioning for Testing and Troubleshooting

On-demand provisioning lets you trigger a single-user sync cycle without waiting for the next scheduled increment. Use it during testing of attribute mappings, scoping filter changes, or after resolving a quarantine condition.

# Trigger on-demand provisioning for a single user (useful during testing)
# Requires the provisioning job ID — get it from the Enterprise App -> Provisioning blade
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'SynchronizationData-User.Upload'

# Get the service principal ID for the target app
$sp = Get-MgServicePrincipal -Filter "displayName eq 'Salesforce'" | Select-Object -First 1

# List provisioning jobs for this SP
$jobs = Get-MgServicePrincipalSynchronizationJob -ServicePrincipalId $sp.Id
$jobId = $jobs[0].Id

# Trigger on-demand provisioning for a specific user UPN
$userId = (Get-MgUser -Filter "userPrincipalName eq 'alice@contoso.com'").Id

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
  -Uri "/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$($sp.Id)/synchronization/jobs/$jobId/provisionOnDemand" `
  -Body (@{subjects = @(@{objectId = $userId; objectTypeName = 'User'})} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5)

On-demand provisioning is a P2 feature. The regular provisioning cycle for all in-scope users runs on a 40-minute increment by default and cannot be manually accelerated beyond on-demand single-user triggers.

The manager attribute is commonly misconfigured: the source system may export a manager who is not yet provisioned in the target, causing a referential integrity error. Detect these with a focused KQL query before they cascade into org-chart gaps in downstream apps.

// Manager attribute sync failures — common cause of org-chart gaps in downstream apps
AADProvisioningLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where Result == 'Failure'
| mv-expand ModifiedProperties
| where tostring(ModifiedProperties.Name) == 'manager'
| extend SourceUPN  = tostring(SourceIdentity.UserPrincipalName)
| extend ErrorCode  = tostring(StatusInfo.errorCode)
| extend TargetApp  = tostring(ProvisioningServicePrincipalDisplayName)
| project TimeGenerated, SourceUPN, TargetApp, ErrorCode
| order by TimeGenerated desc
Provisioning Logs record every action of the Entra provisioning engine: inbound HR sync, outbound SaaS provisioning, and on-premises write-back.
Key result states: Success (normal), Failure (retry pending), Skipped (scoping filter or no delta), Quarantine (repeated failures — needs manual intervention).
Enable the ProvisioningLogs diagnostic category separately from AuditLogs and SignInLogs in the diagnostic setting.
Use the AADProvisioningLogs table in Log Analytics for KQL queries: error rate trending, quarantine detection, skip reason analysis, and attribute change audit.
Build Sentinel analytics rules to alert on elevated error rates before they escalate to a full quarantine — proactive monitoring avoids user access gaps.
Common failure causes: missing permissions on the provisioning SP, schema mismatches, SCIM throttling, and null required attributes from the HR source.
Always export failures programmatically via Graph API or KQL for offline root-cause analysis — the portal UI limits result depth for complex investigations.
